title: Apply for a Student Oyster card early
text: Use your university email to apply online and save money on London transport.
author: London Student

title: Shop at Aldi or Lidl
text: These supermarkets are usually cheaper than Tesco or Sainsbury’s for everyday groceries.
author: UK Student

title: Check bills before renting
text: Always ask if electricity, gas, and internet are included in rent.
author: International Student

title: Find halal food near universities
text: Most UK cities have halal shops near campuses. Search Google Maps or ask student societies.
author: Manchester Student

title: Join university societies
text: Clubs help you make friends quickly and reduce homesickness.
author: Campus Mentor

title: Use student discount apps
text: Download UNiDAYS and Student Beans for discounts in shops and online stores.
author: Student Advisor

title: Get a Railcard
text: A 16–25 or student railcard gives up to 1/3 off train tickets.
author: UK Traveller

title: Cook more instead of eating out
text: Restaurant meals are expensive. Cooking at home saves a lot each month.
author: Boston Student

title: Share accommodation
text: Sharing apartments with other students reduces rent and utility costs.
author: New York Student

title: Check campus shuttle services
text: Many universities offer free transport between campus buildings and housing areas.
author: Campus Guide

title: Join cultural student groups
text: International societies often share locations of affordable cultural restaurants.
author: International Club

title: Attend orientation events
text: Orientation week helps you understand campus systems and meet new people.
author: Student Volunteer

title: Use student ID everywhere
text: Many museums, cinemas, and shops offer discounts with a student ID.
author: Exchange Student

title: Ask about heating costs
text: Heating can be expensive in winter, so confirm if it’s included in rent.
author: Toronto Student

title: Get a student transit pass
text: Most cities offer discounted monthly passes for students.
author: Vancouver Student

title: Buy winter clothes locally
text: It’s cheaper to buy proper winter jackets after arriving in Canada.
author: International Student

title: Use community markets
text: Local markets often sell fresh food cheaper than supermarkets.
author: Montreal Student

title: Volunteer to build connections
text: Volunteering helps improve experience and meet local people.
author: Student Helper

title: Check winter bus schedules
text: Snow can affect transport times, so always plan extra travel time.
author: Calgary Student

title: Use student banking accounts
text: Many Canadian banks offer zero-fee accounts for students.
author: Finance Student
